page: Amazon Web Services x2iedn.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8375C processor, delivering 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores with a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 3072.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 32.0 GB of RAM per physical core. This high memory-to-vCPU ratio positions the instance for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

The instance is equipped with 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and offers a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory bandwidth, with large-block read-only memory bandwidth reaching 296.57 GB/sec. It also exhibits top-tier performance in cryptographic operations, as evidenced by its PassMark CPU encryption test results, alongside strong performance in database operations and Redis SET operations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the x2iedn.24xlarge offers high resource density, combining massive memory capacity with fast local storage and high network throughput, which reduces the need for multi-node clustering for large datasets. However, the absence of dedicated GPU hardware limits its utility for heavy deep learning training workloads. This instance is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, high-performance relational databases, electronic design automation, and intensive data analytics pipelines.
description: Amazon Web Services x2iedn.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. Powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 96 vCPUs, 48 physical cores, and a 3.5 GHz clock speed. The instance is equipped with 3072.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing 32.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage alongside a 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data highlights strong multi-core performance, top-tier cryptographic execution, and strong database operation throughput. While highly cost-efficient for consolidating massive datasets into a single node, the lack of GPU acceleration limits its suitability for deep learning. It is best suited for running large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise relational databases, and memory-bound data analytics.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x2iedn.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 3072 GB of DDR4 RAM, 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth for intensive database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x2iedn.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 96 vCPUs, 3 TB of RAM, and 2.85 TB NVMe SSD storage for enterprise databases.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized server featuring three terabytes of RAM, dedicated Intel Xeon processors, and fast local NVMe storage for enterprise databases.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz
3072 GB of DDR4 memory providing 32 GB of RAM per physical core
2850 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for data transfer
Strong benchmark performance in database and cryptographic operations
Designed for large-scale in-memory databases and data analytics
